seo.primaryKeyword: how many questions can you miss on AP Psych
seo.title: How Many Questions Can You Miss on AP Psych and Still Get a 5?
seo.description: Find out how many AP Psych multiple-choice questions you may be able to miss and still earn a 3, 4, or 5 using estimated score ranges.
seo.h1: How Many Questions Can You Miss on AP Psych?

content.directAnswer: How many questions you can miss depends on your FRQ score and target AP score. Use the calculator to test missed-question scenarios.

sections.heading: Missed Questions Are Only Part of the Score
sections.body: A missed-question estimate is useful, but your final estimate also depends on FRQ performance.

sections.heading: Common Missed-Question Scenarios
sections.body: Try scenarios such as missing 10, 15, 20, or 25 MCQ questions and compare the results with different FRQ scores.

faqs.question: Is this calculator official?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is an independent estimate and is not affiliated with or endorsed by College Board.

faqs.question: Can actual AP score cutoffs vary?
faqs.answer: Yes. Actual score thresholds can vary by year and exam form, so this tool should be used for planning only.

faqs.question: Does FRQ performance matter for AP Psych?
faqs.answer: Yes. FRQ performance contributes to the composite estimate, so a stronger FRQ score can offset some missed multiple-choice questions.

faqs.question: Should I use this before or after the exam?
faqs.answer: You can use it before the exam for target planning or after the exam to estimate possible score outcomes.
